channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Federal workers start to get a new email demanding their accomplishments
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Federal employees are starting to receive another email requiring them to explain their recent accomplishments, a renewed attempt by President Donald Trump and billionaire entrepreneur Elon Musk to demand answers from the government workforce.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelensky won’t apologize for Oval Office spat, says it was bad for both sides
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky wouldn’t apologize for the meeting with President Trump, which erupted into a remarkable and tense spat, arguing that the situation was bad for both countries.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump and Zelenskyy through the years: From a 'perfect' call to an Oval Office meltdown
item.description: The first time Donald Trump spoke to Volodymyr Zelenskyy, he tried to pressure Ukraine’s new leader to dig up dirt on Joe Biden ahead of the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Texas GOP leaders defend appointee after tense exchange over diversity hiring
item.description: Several of Texas’ top Republicans are defending Gov. Greg Abbott’s pick to head the state’s water agency following pointed questions from a GOP lawmaker over the agency’s diversity hiring policies.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: European leaders support Ukraine amid tensions with Trump
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — European leaders pledged late Friday to stand by Ukraine in the wake of the contentious Oval Office meeting between U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump administration approves major nearly $3 billion arms sale to Israel
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has approved a major nearly $3 billion arms sale to Israel, bypassing a normal congressional review to provide the country with more of the 2,000-pound bombs that it has used in the war against Hamas in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Europe needs to 'step up' or Zelenskyy needs to 'step down': What's next after explosive Oval Office meeting
item.description: Europe either needs to "step up" or Zelenskyy needs to "step down" after testy Oval Office meeting, according to Ukraine observers who saw the fiery exchange at the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's top prosecutor in DC demotes several supervisors who handled politically sensitive cases
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Several senior leaders in Washington's federal prosecutors office have been demoted to jobs handling misdemeanors or other low-level matters, in the latest move by President Donald Trump's U.S. attorney to roil the office that oversaw the massive prosecution of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, ERIC TUCKER and BYRON TAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Epstein files debacle spurs new interest in contents of promised RFK, MLK assassination files
item.description: In accordance with President Trump's executive order, the plan to release the MLK and RFK files is due on March 9, just weeks after the DOJ revealed highly anticipated Epstein files, many of which had already been released.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WATCH: Zelenskyy's White House meeting with Trump, Vance turns to shouting match
item.description: A White House meeting that was intended to kick off negotiations over a deal that would offer the United States access to Ukraine's rare earth minerals unraveled Friday afternoon as Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy clashed with President Donald Trump and Vice President JD Vance in the Oval Of
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, ZEKE MILLER and JUSTIN SPIKE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here's who Biden blames for his 2024 election loss, Trump divulges after private convo
item.description: President Donald Trump said former President Joe Biden blamed former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi and former President Barack Obama for the election loss in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump, Vance go off on Zelensky in contentious Oval Office spat
item.description: President Trump went off on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky during an extraordinary Oval Office meeting in front of cameras on Friday in which he raised his voice and called the foreign leader “disrespectful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Most Americans who experienced severe winter weather see climate change at work, AP-NORC poll shows
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Matt Ries has lived in Florida only three years, but everyone told him last summer was unusually hot. That was followed by three hurricanes in close succession. Then temperatures dropped below freezing for days this winter, and snow blanketed part of the state.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER and AMELIA THOMSON-DeVEAUX, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Those with gender dysphoria can no longer serve in military: Pentagon
item.description: (NewsNation) — A memo sent to Defense Department leaders Thursday states those with gender dysphoria are no longer eligible for military service. This document, written by Darin S.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he plans to add White House ballroom
item.description: President Trump in a new interview said he is planning to pave part of the Rose Garden, similar to a patio at his Mar-a-Lago property in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Republican senators threaten not to boost Texas public universities’ funding over DEI ban
item.description: In a letter, Sens. Brandon Creighton and Paul Bettencourt said they found “numerous” violations of the law, but didn’t provide specifics.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jessica Priest, KPRC 2

item.title: Children with disabilities swept up in DEI fight, advocates say
item.description: Children with disabilities are getting swept up in the Trump administration's crusade against diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) and its push to end the Education Department, advocates say.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Candidates for NYC mayor work to get on the ballot as Cuomo looms large
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Tuesday was the first day when New York City mayoral candidates could collect signatures to gain access to the primary ballot in June. Former Gov. Andrew Cuomo continues to be favored in the race for mayor, despite not officially declaring.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: FCC chairman climbs Nexstar broadcast tower to promote industry, jobs
item.description: The nation's top communications regulator made a stop in Baldwin County Monday morning and you might be surprised why.
item.dc:creator: Debbie Williams, Fox 5

item.title: Musk says failure to respond to email a second time will end in termination
item.description: The message appears to contradict recent guidance from the Office of Personnel Management (OPM), which told agency leaders Monday afternoon that employee response to the initial email was not mandatory and that failure to do so would not be considered a resignation.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: No savings expected from nearly 40% of DOGE-canceled contracts
item.description: Nearly 40% of the federal contracts that the Trump administration claims to have canceled as part of its signature cost-cutting program aren’t expected to save the government any money, the administration's own data shows.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: St. Pete lawmaker proposes bill to dive into wastewater, sewage spill concerns
item.description: The devastating impacts of storm surge and flood waters are a harsh reality many Floridians know all too well.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump defends Musk’s ‘genius’ email to federal workers
item.description: President Trump on Monday defended tech billionaire Elon Musk after pushback over his email demanding federal workers list five accomplishments from the past week and in doing so, raising the of risk losing their jobs. “There was a lot of genius in sending it.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5
